files.content: How to Update Panel Firmware Last Updated: April 30, 2026 Audience: All Users Products: AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, AC-Series Panels Overview Firmware updates add new features, improve stability, and are required before a panel can connect to your network. This guide walks you through the firmware update process step-by-step. --- What Is Firmware? Firmware is the software that runs on your access control panel. Think of it like the operating system on your computer—without it, the panel cannot function. Many newly shipped panels arrive without firmware pre-installed. Signs your panel needs firmware: - Panel shows "No firmware installed" in the software - Firmware version displays as "0.0" or "None" - Panel won't connect to the network despite correct cabling - You receive a "Firmware update available" notification --- Prerequisites Before starting: - Access to the control software (AxTraxNG 27.7.1.20 or AxtraxPro 28.0.5.2+) installed on your computer - Physical access to the panel and power supply - Ethernet cable to connect the panel to your computer (direct connection recommended for first-time updates) - 15-30 minutes of uninterrupted time (do not interrupt the update process) - Backup power or UPS (optional but recommended to prevent interruption if power is lost) Supported Panel Models Firmware updates are available for the following access control panels: - AC-215 / AC-215IP — 2-door controller - AC-225 / AC-225IP — 2-4 door controller (Series A and B) - AC-425 / AC-425IP — 4-8 door controller (Series B) - AC-825IP — 4-8 door controller with up to 12 expansion modules Legacy Note: If you have a Series A AC-225 or AC-425 manufactured before 2011, contact support before updating—your panel may require special handling. --- Update Process Step 1: Prepare Your Setup Best practice: Connect the panel directly to your computer with an Ethernet cable instead of through a network switch. This avoids network interruptions during the update. 1. Locate your panel and the Ethernet cable 2. Unplug the Ethernet cable from your panel (if currently connected) 3. Plug the Ethernet cable directly into your computer 4. Plug the other end directly into the panel's network port 5. Verify the panel is powered on (look for LED indicator on the front) --- Step 2: Launch the Software 1. Open your access control software (AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, etc.) 2. Go to the main configuration screen or "Device Management" 3. Look for a "Search" or "Add Device" button (exact name varies by software version) 4. Click to scan for available panels on your network --- Step 3: Locate and Select Your Panel 1. Wait for the scan to complete (typically 10-20 seconds) 2. Your panel should appear in the list with a status or model number 3. Click to select your panel 4. Review the panel information: - Model number - Current firmware version (may show as "0.0" or "Not installed") - Any available updates --- Step 4: Start the Firmware Update 1. Right-click on the panel in the AC Networks tree on the left side of the software 2. Select "Firmware Update" from the context menu 3. A dialog will appear asking "Are you sure you want to update the firmware of the panel?" 4. Do NOT change any settings in this dialog—the panel type and firmware file should be pre-selected 5. Click "OK" to start the firmware download to the panel If you see a warning: "Some panels have incompatible firmware. Are you sure you want to add them?" — Click "Yes" to proceed anyway. This is normal when updating older firmware versions. --- Step 5: Monitor the Update Progress Important: Do NOT disconnect power or unplug the Ethernet cable during this process. 1. After clicking OK, the dialog closes and you return to the main window 2. Select the Network in the left panel (under AC Networks) 3. Look at the "Downloads" column in the right panel—it will show progress like "452 / 472" (bytes downloaded) 4. The download typically takes 3-5 minutes depending on the firmware size 5. Watch the "Status" column—it should show "Download in progress" then change to complete 6. Check the Events log at the bottom for status messages like "Started Firmware update" and "Firmware update" events 7. Wait until the status shows the download is finished Successful update indicators: - Status changes from "Download in progress" to normal - Events log shows "Firmware update" event with the panel name - Downloads column stops incrementing - Panel automatically restarts (you may see a brief connectivity interruption) If you see errors in the Events log: - "Invalid Firmware" — The firmware file may be corrupted or incompatible. Try the update again - "Connection Lost" — Restart the panel and try again with a direct Ethernet connection - "Wrong value is ac225v05_00_14" — Firmware version mismatch. Verify you're using the correct firmware for your panel model --- Step 6: Verify the Update 1. Once the update completes, the panel will show a new firmware version (not "0.0") 2. Restart the panel (unplug and plug back in) 3. Reconnect to your network (if you used a direct connection, now return the Ethernet cable to your network) 4. Search for the panel again in the software to confirm it appears on your network 5. You should now be able to configure the panel normally --- Troubleshooting Update Issues "Update Fails" or "Connection Lost" Cause: The connection was interrupted (network disconnection, power loss, or software crash) Fix: 1. Restart the panel (unplug for 30 seconds, then plug back in) 2. Try a direct Ethernet connection to your computer (not through a network switch) 3. Attempt the firmware update again 4. If it continues to fail, try on a different computer "Panel Disappears During Update" Cause: The panel lost power or network connectivity mid-update Fix: 1. Check that the power supply is firmly connected 2. Check that the Ethernet cable is fully inserted 3. Restart both the panel and your software 4. Try the update again with a direct Ethernet connection "Firmware Version Doesn't Change" Cause: The update may not have completed fully Fix: 1. Manually restart the panel (unplug, wait 30 seconds, plug back in) 2. Open the software and check the firmware version again 3. If still showing old version, attempt the update a second time --- After Your Update Next Steps 1. Configure the panel with your facility code, readers, doors, and cards 2. Test all connected readers to ensure they're responding 3. Verify all doors unlock/lock properly 4. Schedule a follow-up firmware check in 6 months to stay current with security patches Regular Updates - Check for firmware updates every 6

files.content: # How to Update Panel Firmware **Last Updated:** April 30, 2026 **Audience:** All Users **Products:** AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, AC-Series Panels ## Overview Firmware updates add new features, improve stability, and are **required** before a panel can connect to your network. This guide walks you through the firmware update process step-by-step. --- ## What Is Firmware? Firmware is the software that runs on your access control panel. Think of it like the operating system on your computer—without it, the panel cannot function. Many newly shipped panels arrive without firmware pre-installed. **Signs your panel needs firmware:** - Panel shows "No firmware installed" in the software - Firmware version displays as "0.0" or "None" - Panel won't connect to the network despite correct cabling - You receive a "Firmware update available" notification --- ## Prerequisites Before starting: - **Access to the control software** (AxTraxNG 27.7.1.20 or AxtraxPro 28.0.5.2+) installed on your computer - **Physical access to the panel** and power supply - **Ethernet cable** to connect the panel to your computer (direct connection recommended for first-time updates) - **15-30 minutes** of uninterrupted time (do not interrupt the update process) - **Backup power or UPS** (optional but recommended to prevent interruption if power is lost) ### Supported Panel Models Firmware updates are available for the following access control panels: - **AC-215 / AC-215IP** — 2-door controller - **AC-225 / AC-225IP** — 2-4 door controller (Series A and B) - **AC-425 / AC-425IP** — 4-8 door controller (Series B) - **AC-825IP** — 4-8 door controller with up to 12 expansion modules **Legacy Note:** If you have a Series A AC-225 or AC-425 manufactured before 2011, contact support before updating—your panel may require special handling. --- ## Update Process ### Step 1: Prepare Your Setup **Best practice:** Connect the panel directly to your computer with an Ethernet cable instead of through a network switch. This avoids network interruptions during the update. 1. Locate your panel and the Ethernet cable 2. Unplug the Ethernet cable from your panel (if currently connected) 3. **Plug the Ethernet cable directly into your computer** 4. **Plug the other end directly into the panel's network port** 5. Verify the panel is powered on (look for LED indicator on the front) --- ### Step 2: Launch the Software 1. **Open your access control software** (AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, etc.) 2. **Go to the main configuration screen** or "Device Management" 3. **Look for a "Search" or "Add Device" button** (exact name varies by software version) 4. Click to scan for available panels on your network --- ### Step 3: Locate and Select Your Panel 1. **Wait for the scan to complete** (typically 10-20 seconds) 2. **Your panel should appear in the list** with a status or model number 3. **Click to select your panel** 4. **Review the panel information:** - Model number - Current firmware version (may show as "0.0" or "Not installed") - Any available updates --- ### Step 4: Start the Firmware Update 1. **Right-click on the panel** in the AC Networks tree on the left side of the software 2. **Select "Firmware Update"** from the context menu 3. **A dialog will appear** asking "Are you sure you want to update the firmware of the panel?" 4. **Do NOT change any settings** in this dialog—the panel type and firmware file should be pre-selected 5. **Click "OK"** to start the firmware download to the panel **If you see a warning:** "Some panels have incompatible firmware. Are you sure you want to add them?" — **Click "Yes"** to proceed anyway. This is normal when updating older firmware versions. --- ### Step 5: Monitor the Update Progress **Important:** Do NOT disconnect power or unplug the Ethernet cable during this process. 1. **After clicking OK, the dialog closes and you return to the main window** 2. **Select the Network in the left panel** (under AC Networks) 3. **Look at the "Downloads" column** in the right panel—it will show progress like "452 / 472" (bytes downloaded) 4. **The download typically takes 3-5 minutes** depending on the firmware size 5. **Watch the "Status" column**—it should show "Download in progress" then change to complete 6. **Check the Events log at the bottom** for status messages like "Started Firmware update" and "Firmware update" events 7. **Wait until the status shows the download is finished** **Successful update indicators:** - Status changes from "Download in progress" to normal - Events log shows "Firmware update" event with the panel name - Downloads column stops incrementing - Panel automatically restarts (you may see a brief connectivity interruption) **If you see errors in the Events log:** - **"Invalid Firmware"** — The firmware file may be corrupted or incompatible. Try the update again - **"Connection Lost"** — Restart the panel and try again with a direct Ethernet connection - **"Wrong value is ac225v05_00_14"** — Firmware version mismatch. Verify you're using the correct firmware for your panel model --- ### Step 6: Verify the Update 1. **Once the update completes**, the panel will show a new firmware version (not "0.0") 2. **Restart the panel** (unplug and plug back in) 3. **Reconnect to your network** (if you used a direct connection, now return the Ethernet cable to your network) 4. **Search for the panel again** in the software to confirm it appears on your network 5. **You should now be able to configure the panel normally** --- ## Troubleshooting Update Issues ### "Update Fails" or "Connection Lost" **Cause:** The connection was interrupted (network disconnection, power loss, or software crash) **Fix:** 1. Restart the panel (unplug for 30 seconds, then plug back in) 2. Try a direct Ethernet connection to your computer (not through a network switch) 3. Attempt the firmware update again 4. If it continues to fail, try on a different computer ### "Panel Disappears During Update" **Cause:** The panel lost power or network connectivity mid-update **Fix:** 1. Check that the power supply is firmly connected 2. Check that the Ethernet cable is fully inserted 3. Restart both the panel and your software 4. Try the update again with a direct Ethernet connection ### "Firmware Version Doesn't Change" **Cause:** The update may not have completed fully **Fix:** 1. Manually restart the panel (unplug, wait 30 seconds, plug back in) 2. Open the software and check the firmware version again 3. If still showing old version, attempt the update a second time --- ## After Your Update ### Next Steps 1. **Configure the panel** with your facility code, readers, doors, and cards 2. **Test all connected readers** to ensure they're responding 3. **Verify all doors unlock/lock properly** 4. **Schedule a follow-up firmware check** in 6 months to stay current with security patches ### Regular Updates - **Check for firmware updates** every 6 months - **New firmware versions** may include security improvements and bug fixes - **Keep your documentation current** with the firmware version in use --- ## Prevention - **Update firmware immediately** when you receive a new panel - **Don't skip firmware updates** thinking the panel will work without it - **Use quality power supplies** to prevent interruptions during updates - **Back up your panel configuration** before major updates (some updates reset settings) - **Update during low-usage hours** to minimize disruption if something goes wrong --- ## Still Having Issues? If your firmware update isn't working: - **Note the panel model number** and current firmware version - **Document the exact error message** you received - **Check your software version** (Help > About) - **Try a direct Ethernet connection** instead of through your network - **Contact support** with this information --- ## Related Articles - [Panel Not Connecting to Network](KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md) - [Software Installation & Setup Guide](software-install.md) - [Troubleshooting: Panel Communication Errors](advanced-troubleshooting.md) --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | |------|--------| | Apr 30, 2026 | Initial publication based on 18 support tickets |

files.content: How to Add, Manage, and Troubleshoot Access Cards and Credentials Last Updated: April 30, 2026 Audience: All Users Products: AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, AC-Series Panels Overview This guide covers how to add new access cards to your system, manage credentials, and resolve common issues like cards not working or readers rejecting valid cards. --- What Are Cards and Credentials? Card: A physical credential (badge, key fob, card, or tag) that users swipe or tap on a reader to grant access. Credential: The digital record in your software that associates a card ID with a user and their access permissions (which doors they can open, what times, etc.). Reader: The device mounted near a door that reads the card and communicates with the panel. --- Part 1: Adding a New Access Card Step 1: Prepare the Card Information Before you add a card, gather: - Card ID number (often printed on the back of the card or key fob) - User name (who the card belongs to) - Card type (standard access card, biometric card, proximity card, etc.) - Facility code (optional — identifies which facility this card is for, if using multiple locations) - Access permissions (which doors they can open, time restrictions, etc.) How to find the card ID: - Many cards have the ID printed on the back (a long number) - If not visible, you can "program" the card by tapping it on a reader in programming mode - Check your card supplier documentation for the ID format --- Step 2: Add the Card to the Software 1. Open your access control software (AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, etc.) 2. Navigate to "Users," "Cards," or "Credentials" (varies by software version) 3. Click "Add New Card" or the "+" button 4. Fill in the card information: - Card ID (the number on the card) - User name - Facility code (if required) - Start and end dates (when the card is active) - Access level or group (which doors they can open) 5. Click "Save" or "Add Card" 6. The software will display a confirmation message --- Step 3: Sync with Panel For the card to work, the panel must receive the new credential. 1. Select the panel from the device list in your software 2. Click "Upload Configuration," "Sync," or "Send to Panel" 3. Wait for the sync to complete (typically 10-30 seconds) 4. You should see a "Sync Complete" or "Success" message --- Step 4: Test the Card 1. Go to the reader that corresponds to the door you gave access to 2. Swipe or tap the new card on the reader 3. The reader should beep or light up (behavior varies by reader type) 4. The door should unlock (if it's the right time and the credential is active) 5. If it doesn't work, go to Part 3: Troubleshooting --- Part 2: Managing Existing Cards Disabling a Card (Without Deleting) If an employee leaves but you want to keep their record: 1. Go to Users > Cards or Credentials 2. Find the card in the list 3. Click to edit it 4. Change the "Status" to "Inactive" or "Disabled" 5. Set the "End Date" to today (optional but recommended) 6. Click "Save" 7. Sync the change to the panel (Upload Configuration) The card will no longer grant access, but the record remains in your system. Reassigning a Card to a New User 1. Go to Users > Cards or Credentials 2. Find the card 3. Click to edit it 4. Change the "User Name" to the new person 5. Update access permissions if needed 6. Click "Save" 7. Sync to the panel The card is now assigned to the new user. Updating Card Access Permissions 1. Go to Users > Cards 2. Find the card 3. Edit the "Access Group" or "Doors" field 4. Select which doors they should have access to 5. Set time restrictions if needed (e.g., "Only weekdays 8am-6pm") 6. Click "Save" 7. Sync to the panel --- Part 3: Troubleshooting Card Issues Issue: Card Won't Work After Adding It Symptoms: Card was just added but doesn't grant access. Reader beeps but door doesn't unlock. Cause: The configuration wasn't synced to the panel, or the card was added but not activated. Fix: 1. Check the card status in software — it should show as "Active" 2. Verify the card end date hasn't already passed 3. Sync the configuration to the panel again (Upload Configuration / Sync) 4. Wait 30 seconds for the sync to reach the readers 5. Try the card again --- Issue: New Codes Are Not Working Symptoms: After adding new access codes in the software, the panel doesn't recognize them. Cause: The panel firmware may need updating, or the synchronization failed. Fix: 1. Verify the panel firmware is current (if showing version 0.0 or very old, update it first) 2. Check that the panel is connected to the network (see [Panel Connectivity](KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md)) 3. Manually resync the configuration: - Select the panel - Click "Upload Configuration" or "Sync" - Wait for completion 4. If sync fails, check the error message: - "Panel Unreachable" — Panel lost network connection (restart it) - "Database Error" — Try syncing again, or restart your software 5. Try the card again after successful sync --- Issue: Biometric Reader Sends Same ID for All Cards Symptoms: No matter what card is swiped on a biometric reader, it sends the same facility code and card ID (usually "0" and "9999"). Cause: The biometric reader is not configured properly or lost its configuration. Fix: 1. Check the reader settings in your software: - Go to Devices > Readers (or similar) - Find the biometric reader - Verify it's set to "Biometric mode" or "Card read mode" (not demo mode) 2. If the setting shows wrong, update it: - Change the mode to the correct setting - Click "Save" or "Apply" - Sync to the panel 3. If still showing wrong IDs, the reader may have lost its configuration: - Power cycle the reader (unplug for 30 seconds, plug back in) - Try swiping the card again 4. If issue persists, contact your reader's manufacturer or support team --- Issue: Cards Don't Stick on User Account Symptoms: You add a card to a user, but when you open the user record later, the card is gone or shows as "unassigned." Cause: This usually occurs if: - The save didn't complete properly - There's a database connection issue - The user has too many cards assigned (some systems have limits) Fix: 1. Try adding the card again: - Open the user record - Click "Add Card" - Select the card from the list - Click "Save" - Do not navigate away until you see a success message 2. If it still doesn't stick, restart your software: - Close the software completely - Reopen it - Go to the user and try adding the card again 3. If using a PC that's been running for a while, restart the computer: - This can resolve temporary database connection issues - After restart, try adding the card again 4. Check your panel's database isn't full: - Some systems have a limit on the number of cards - Contact support if you're hitting limits --- Issue: "Cannot Find Facility Code" or "Error Creating Card Design" Symptoms: Error message appears when trying to add a card, saying "Facility code not found" or "Cannot edit card design." Cause: The facility code associated with your panel hasn't been configured yet. The facility code is a required identifier that tells all readers which system they belong to. Fix: 1. Find your panel's facility code: - Go to Devices > Panels or Device Configuration - Find your panel in the list (look for AC-215, AC-225, AC-425, or AC-825) - Look for a "Facility Code," "Site Code," or "FC" field - If blank or showing "0," you need to set one 2. Set the facility code: - Click to edit the panel - Enter a facility code (typically a number between 0-255) - This is usually a number provided by your installer, in your documentation, or printed on the back of your access cards - Click "Save" 3. Sync the configuration to the panel: - Select the panel - Click "Upload Configuration," "Send to Panel," or "Sync" - Wait for completion (typically 10-30 seconds) 4. Try adding the card again Card Format Information: Your access cards may use different formats depending on configuration: - Standard 26-bit format: Uses facility code + card number (most common) - No facility code (26-bit): Uses only card number without facility code (specialized installations) If you don't know your facility code: - Check your installation documentation or system notes - Contact your system installer - Look at the back of existing access cards (often printed there) - Check with your security manager or facilities team --- Panel Card Capacity Reference Different panel models support different numbers of cards and users: If you're hitting capacity limits on storing events or users, you may need to: - Archive old event logs - Migrate to a higher-capacity panel (AC-825IP) - Disable automatic event logging for non-critical readers --- Prevention - Document your facility code and keep it in an a

files.content: # How to Add, Manage, and Troubleshoot Access Cards and Credentials **Last Updated:** April 30, 2026 **Audience:** All Users **Products:** AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, AC-Series Panels ## Overview This guide covers how to add new access cards to your system, manage credentials, and resolve common issues like cards not working or readers rejecting valid cards. --- ## What Are Cards and Credentials? **Card:** A physical credential (badge, key fob, card, or tag) that users swipe or tap on a reader to grant access. **Credential:** The digital record in your software that associates a card ID with a user and their access permissions (which doors they can open, what times, etc.). **Reader:** The device mounted near a door that reads the card and communicates with the panel. --- ## Part 1: Adding a New Access Card ### Step 1: Prepare the Card Information Before you add a card, gather: - **Card ID number** (often printed on the back of the card or key fob) - **User name** (who the card belongs to) - **Card type** (standard access card, biometric card, proximity card, etc.) - **Facility code** (optional — identifies which facility this card is for, if using multiple locations) - **Access permissions** (which doors they can open, time restrictions, etc.) **How to find the card ID:** - Many cards have the ID printed on the back (a long number) - If not visible, you can "program" the card by tapping it on a reader in programming mode - Check your card supplier documentation for the ID format --- ### Step 2: Add the Card to the Software 1. **Open your access control software** (AxTraxNG, AxtraxPro, etc.) 2. **Navigate to "Users," "Cards," or "Credentials"** (varies by software version) 3. **Click "Add New Card" or the "+" button** 4. **Fill in the card information:** - Card ID (the number on the card) - User name - Facility code (if required) - Start and end dates (when the card is active) - Access level or group (which doors they can open) 5. **Click "Save" or "Add Card"** 6. **The software will display a confirmation** message --- ### Step 3: Sync with Panel For the card to work, the panel must receive the new credential. 1. **Select the panel** from the device list in your software 2. **Click "Upload Configuration," "Sync," or "Send to Panel"** 3. **Wait for the sync to complete** (typically 10-30 seconds) 4. **You should see a "Sync Complete" or "Success" message** --- ### Step 4: Test the Card 1. **Go to the reader** that corresponds to the door you gave access to 2. **Swipe or tap the new card** on the reader 3. **The reader should beep or light up** (behavior varies by reader type) 4. **The door should unlock** (if it's the right time and the credential is active) 5. **If it doesn't work, go to Part 3: Troubleshooting** --- ## Part 2: Managing Existing Cards ### Disabling a Card (Without Deleting) If an employee leaves but you want to keep their record: 1. **Go to Users > Cards** or **Credentials** 2. **Find the card in the list** 3. **Click to edit it** 4. **Change the "Status" to "Inactive" or "Disabled"** 5. **Set the "End Date" to today** (optional but recommended) 6. **Click "Save"** 7. **Sync the change to the panel** (Upload Configuration) The card will no longer grant access, but the record remains in your system. ### Reassigning a Card to a New User 1. **Go to Users > Cards** or **Credentials** 2. **Find the card** 3. **Click to edit it** 4. **Change the "User Name" to the new person** 5. **Update access permissions** if needed 6. **Click "Save"** 7. **Sync to the panel** The card is now assigned to the new user. ### Updating Card Access Permissions 1. **Go to Users > Cards** 2. **Find the card** 3. **Edit the "Access Group" or "Doors"** field 4. **Select which doors they should have access to** 5. **Set time restrictions if needed** (e.g., "Only weekdays 8am-6pm") 6. **Click "Save"** 7. **Sync to the panel** --- ## Part 3: Troubleshooting Card Issues ### Issue: Card Won't Work After Adding It **Symptoms:** Card was just added but doesn't grant access. Reader beeps but door doesn't unlock. **Cause:** The configuration wasn't synced to the panel, or the card was added but not activated. **Fix:** 1. **Check the card status in software** — it should show as "Active" 2. **Verify the card end date** hasn't already passed 3. **Sync the configuration to the panel again** (Upload Configuration / Sync) 4. **Wait 30 seconds** for the sync to reach the readers 5. **Try the card again** --- ### Issue: New Codes Are Not Working **Symptoms:** After adding new access codes in the software, the panel doesn't recognize them. **Cause:** The panel firmware may need updating, or the synchronization failed. **Fix:** 1. **Verify the panel firmware is current** (if showing version 0.0 or very old, update it first) 2. **Check that the panel is connected to the network** (see [Panel Connectivity](KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md)) 3. **Manually resync the configuration:** - Select the panel - Click "Upload Configuration" or "Sync" - Wait for completion 4. **If sync fails**, check the error message: - **"Panel Unreachable"** — Panel lost network connection (restart it) - **"Database Error"** — Try syncing again, or restart your software 5. **Try the card again after successful sync** --- ### Issue: Biometric Reader Sends Same ID for All Cards **Symptoms:** No matter what card is swiped on a biometric reader, it sends the same facility code and card ID (usually "0" and "9999"). **Cause:** The biometric reader is not configured properly or lost its configuration. **Fix:** 1. **Check the reader settings in your software:** - Go to Devices > Readers (or similar) - Find the biometric reader - Verify it's set to "Biometric mode" or "Card read mode" (not demo mode) 2. **If the setting shows wrong, update it:** - Change the mode to the correct setting - Click "Save" or "Apply" - Sync to the panel 3. **If still showing wrong IDs**, the reader may have lost its configuration: - Power cycle the reader (unplug for 30 seconds, plug back in) - Try swiping the card again 4. **If issue persists**, contact your reader's manufacturer or support team --- ### Issue: Cards Don't Stick on User Account **Symptoms:** You add a card to a user, but when you open the user record later, the card is gone or shows as "unassigned." **Cause:** This usually occurs if: - The save didn't complete properly - There's a database connection issue - The user has too many cards assigned (some systems have limits) **Fix:** 1. **Try adding the card again:** - Open the user record - Click "Add Card" - Select the card from the list - Click "Save" - **Do not navigate away** until you see a success message 2. **If it still doesn't stick**, restart your software: - Close the software completely - Reopen it - Go to the user and try adding the card again 3. **If using a PC that's been running for a while**, restart the computer: - This can resolve temporary database connection issues - After restart, try adding the card again 4. **Check your panel's database isn't full:** - Some systems have a limit on the number of cards - Contact support if you're hitting limits --- ### Issue: "Cannot Find Facility Code" or "Error Creating Card Design" **Symptoms:** Error message appears when trying to add a card, saying "Facility code not found" or "Cannot edit card design." **Cause:** The facility code associated with your panel hasn't been configured yet. The facility code is a required identifier that tells all readers which system they belong to. **Fix:** 1. **Find your panel's facility code:** - Go to Devices > Panels or Device Configuration - Find your panel in the list (look for AC-215, AC-225, AC-425, or AC-825) - Look for a "Facility Code," "Site Code," or "FC" field - If blank or showing "0," you need to set one 2. **Set the facility code:** - Click to edit the panel - Enter a facility code (typically a number between 0-255) - This is usually a number provided by your installer, in your documentation, or printed on the back of your access cards - Click "Save" 3. **Sync the configuration to the panel:** - Select the panel - Click "Upload Configuration," "Send to Panel," or "Sync" - Wait for completion (typically 10-30 seconds) 4. **Try adding the card again** **Card Format Information:** Your access cards may use different formats depending on configuration: - **Standard 26-bit format:** Uses facility code + card number (most common) - **No facility code (26-bit):** Uses only card number without facility code (specialized installations) **If you don't know your facility code:** - Check your installation documentation or system notes - Contact your system installer - Look at the back of existing access cards (often printed there) - Check with your security manager or facilities team --- ## Panel Card Capacity Reference Different panel models support different numbers of cards and users: | Panel Model | Max Users | Max Stored Events | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | **AC-215/225** | 30,000 users | 20,000 events | Entry-level 2-4 door panels | | **AC-425** | 30,000 users | 20,000 events | Mid-range 4-8 door panels | | **AC-825IP** | 60,000 users | 500,000 events | High-capacity with expansion support | If you're hitting capacity limits on storing events or users, you may need to: - Archive old event logs - Migrate to a higher-capacity panel (AC-825IP) - Disable automatic event logging for non-critical readers --- ## Prevention - **Document your facility code** and keep it in an accessible location - **Create a naming convention** for access groups (e.g., "Building A - All Doors" vs "Building A - Lobby Only") - **Review access monthly** — remove cards for departed employees promptly and disable rather than delete - **Test new cards immediately** after adding them to catch issues early - **Keep a log** of who has access to sensitive doors and when access was granted - **Use biometric readers** in high-security areas to reduce unauthorized card sharing - **Monitor your card count** — if you approach panel capacity limits, plan for expansion - **Back up your database regularly** — see your backup guide for procedures --- ## Still Having Issues? If you're still experiencing problems: - **Note the specific card ID** that's not working - **Document the reader type** (model number if possible) - **Note the error message** you received (with a screenshot if possible) - **Confirm the panel is connected** to the network - **Check the panel firmware version** (may need updating) - **Contact support** with this information --- ## Related Articles - [Panel Not Connecting to Network](KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md) - [How to Update Panel Firmware](KB_02_Firmware_Updates.md) - [Reader Configuration Guide](reader-setup.md) - [Troubleshooting Reader Errors & Beeping](reader-troubleshooting.md) - [Access Group & Permission Management](access-groups.md) --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | |------|--------| | Apr 30, 2026 | Initial publication based on 22 support tickets |

files.content: Rosslare Security — North America Technical Support Knowledge Base Top 5 Support Issues — Resolution Guide April 2026 | Based on 2,000+ Support Tickets Articles in This Document Article 1: Cards / Credentials Not Working in AxTrax NG Article 2: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG Article 3: How to Move AxTrax NG to a New Computer Article 4: Standalone Controller Programming — Factory Reset, Code Enrollment, and Setup Article 5: AxTrax NG — DB Disconnected or Client Not Connecting to Server These articles address the five most frequently reported issues across the Rosslare North America support team from April 2025 through March 2026. Each article is written for installers and system administrators and can be published directly to a customer-facing knowledge base. Troubleshooting: Cards / Credentials Not Working in AxTrax NG Overview If cardholders are presenting their credentials but the door is not unlocking, or if no event is being logged in AxTrax NG when a card is used, this article walks through the four most common causes and how to fix them. Most cases come down to the card not being in the system, the access group not including the correct reader or schedule, the panel being offline, or a Windows Firewall rule blocking communication. Symptoms Card is presented to a reader but the door does not unlock. Reader shows a red LED or triple beep (access denied). No event is logged in AxTrax NG when the card is used — this typically means the panel is offline. Some cards work on a door while others do not. Newly programmed cards are not working. Common Causes and Solutions Cause 1 — Card Is Not in the System The most frequent reason cards fail. A card must be enrolled in AxTrax NG before it can be granted access at any reader. Open AxTrax NG and go to the Cards section in the left navigation tree. Search for the cardholder by name or card number. If the card is NOT listed, you must add it. Go to Cards, click the green + (Add), and enter the card's facility code and card number. Click Save. If the card IS listed, verify its Status is Active and it has not expired. An inactive or expired card will be denied at the reader even if the person exists in the system. Cause 2 — Access Group Does Not Include the Reader or Is Outside the Time Schedule Even if a card is in the system, it will be denied if its Access Group does not include the specific door being tested, or if the current time is outside the group's allowed schedule. In AxTrax NG, go to Groups > Access Groups in the left tree. Open the Access Group assigned to the cardholder. Click the Readers tab. Confirm the door or reader where access is failing is listed and checked. If the door is missing from the Access Group, add it. Also verify the Time Schedule allows access at the current time — a card can be blocked simply because it is outside the allowed hours. Save the Access Group and test the card again. Cause 3 — Panel Is Not Connected to the Software If the panel managing the reader is offline, AxTrax NG cannot push access decisions. Cards will appear to be presented but no door relay will fire. A missing event log is the key indicator here. In AxTrax NG, go to AC Networks in the left tree and expand the panel that the reader is connected to. Check the panel's Status column in the Table View. 'Connected' or 'Initialized' means the panel is online. Any other status (red, grey, 'Disconnected') means the panel is offline. If the panel is offline, resolve the panel connectivity issue first. See Article 2: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG for full steps. Once the panel is back online, re-test the card. Cards cannot be validated by an offline panel — the door relay will not fire. Cause 4 — Windows Firewall or Antivirus Is Blocking Panel Communication AxTrax NG communicates with panels over TCP port 10001. If this port is blocked, panels go offline and cards fail. Third-party antivirus products (McAfee, Symantec) are also a common cause — Rosslare has only tested and supports Windows Defender. On the Windows PC running AxTrax NG, open Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security (Start > search for 'Windows Firewall with Advanced Security'). Click Inbound Rules on the left. Look for a rule named AxTraxNG or one allowing TCP port 10001. If the rule is missing or shows as disabled, click New Rule > Port > TCP > Specific Local Ports: 10001 > Allow the Connection > apply to all profiles > name it AxTraxNG. Also check if any third-party antivirus (McAfee, Symantec, etc.) is installed. Rosslare only supports Windows Defender. Third-party antivirus must be disabled or have exceptions configured for AxTrax NG. Restart the AxTraxNG Server service (services.msc) and test again. Still Having Issues? Contact Rosslare Technical Support. Have ready: the card number and facility code, the panel model and IP address, a screenshot of the Access Group showing the reader assignment, and the AxTrax NG version (Help > About). Related Articles Article 2: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG Article 5: AxTrax NG — DB Disconnected or Client Not Connecting to Server Troubleshooting: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG Overview This article covers the most common reasons a Rosslare access control panel will not connect to AxTrax NG, and the steps to resolve each one. Panel connectivity issues almost always come down to an IP address mismatch, a network configuration error, a missing expansion board entry in software, a firmware mismatch, or a Windows Firewall rule blocking communication. Network Requirements (Read Before Troubleshooting) Understanding these requirements will help you identify the root cause faster: Same subnet required for initial setup: The AxTrax NG server PC must be on the same physical LAN segment (same subnet) as the panels during initial configuration. Remote/WAN management is supported after initial setup is complete. Static IP addresses required: Both the AxTrax NG server PC and all panels must have static IP addresses. Reserve these addresses in your router so the DHCP server does not assign them to another device. Broadcast and UDP traffic must be allowed: AxTrax NG uses ARP broadcast messages and UDP traffic to configure panel IP addresses. If either of these is blocked by a managed switch or firewall, panel configuration will fail. Antivirus: Only Windows Defender is supported and tested. McAfee, Symantec, and other third-party antivirus products must be disabled or have exceptions configured for AxTrax NG. Do not disable the Windows Firewall: Instead, create a firewall rule for TCP port 10001 as described below. Rosslare Technical Support does not recommend disabling the firewall entirely. Step 1 — Test Network Connectivity (Ping Test) On the Windows PC running AxTrax NG, open Command Prompt (Start > cmd). Type: ping [panel IP address] and press Enter. For example: ping 192.168.1.100 If you receive replies, the PC can reach the panel. Proceed to the 'Check AxTrax NG Panel Properties' section below. If the ping times out, the PC and panel are on different networks or subnets. The AxTrax NG server PC and all panels must be on the same physical LAN segment (same subnet) for initial configuration and normal operation. Step 2 — Use Find Panels to Add or Re-connect a Panel The Find Panels workflow is the correct method for adding a new panel or re-connecting one that has gone offline. Follow these steps exactly: In AxTrax NG, click the + sign next to AxTraxNG in the left Hierarchic View to expand the tree. Click on AC Networks to highlight it. In the Table View, locate your network (e.g., Network 1) and click on it to highlight it. Click the Find Panels icon in the toolbar at the top of the Table View (it looks like a panel with a search symbol). In the Find Panels dialog, click the Find Panels button at the bottom. The software will scan the network. The panel should appear in the list within a few seconds, showing its Description, Panel Type, Panel Status, and Firmware version. If no panel appears, click Find Panels again. You can try up to three times. Once the panel appears, click Stop Find. Check the checkbox next to the panel (or click Select All). Click Add Panels. The panel status will change to Initialized, then to Connected within 1-2 minutes. A Download Count of 3–9 in the bottom status bar is normal during initialization — wait for it to complete. Step 3 — Check Panel IP Address in AxTrax NG Properties If Find Panels cannot locate the panel, the IP stored in AxTrax NG may differ from the panel's actual configured IP. In AxTrax NG, go to AC Networks, expand the network, and right-click the panel that is not connecting. Select Properties from the context menu. In the General tab, verify the IP Address matches the actual IP address configured on the panel's NIC. If they do not match, update the IP address in the panel properties and click Save. Then right-click the network and select Find Panels again. If you are unsure what IP is on the panel, connect a laptop directly to the panel with an Ethernet cable, set the laptop to a static IP in the same range as the panel default (192.168.1.x, subnet 255.255.255.0), and run Find Panels from that laptop. Step 4 — AC-825: Verify D-805 Expansion Board Is Added The AC-825 panel requires the D-805 expansion board to be registered in AxTrax NG. If it is missing, the panel will fail to connect or behave erratically. In AxTrax NG, right-click on the AC-825 panel and select Properties. Go to the Expansion Boards tab. If the D-805 expansion board is not listed, click Add and add the D-805. Also ensure the Windows Firewall rule for TCP port 10001 is in place. Save and run Find Panels again. The panel should come online. Step 5 — Update Panel Firmware The panel firmware must match the version of AxTrax NG software being used. This is required when installing a new panel into an existing system or after a software upgrade. Mismatched firmware/software causes abnormal behavior including failed connections and erratic card reading. In AxTrax NG, expand the network in the left tree and click on the panel name to select it. Click the Update Firmware icon in the toolbar at the top (it shows a panel with an arrow). A dialog will appear showing the Panel Type known on PC and the path to the .hex firmware file. Verify the panel type is correct. If the panel type shown does not match the actual panel hardware, delete the panel from the network, run Find Panels again, and re-add it. Contact Tech Support if it still misidentifies. Click OK to start the firmware update. Do not power off the panel during this process. Firmware must match the AxTrax NG software version. Each version of AxTrax NG includes the correct firmware files for all supported panels. Using mismatched firmware and software versions causes abnormal behavior. Serial Network (MD14U Cable) Troubleshooting If your panels are connected via a serial RS-485 network using the MD14(U) cable rather than Ethernet, follow these additional steps if Find Panels fails: Open Windows Device Manager (Start > Device Manager). Expand Ports (COM & LPT). Note the COM port number assigned to the USB Serial Port (e.g., COM5). In AxTrax NG, go to AC Networks, right-click your serial network, and select Properties. In the General tab, update the COM Port field to match the number shown in Device Manager (e.g., 5). Leave speed at 9600. While running Find Panels, observe the RX LED on the panel. It should flash every 2–3 seconds when data is being received. If the RX LED is NOT flashing, check that the MD14(U) cable is firmly connected and that the RS-232/RS-485 switch on the cable is in the RS-485 position. If the panel still cannot be found, move the MD14(U) cable to a different USB port on the PC, check Device Manager for the new COM port number, update the network setting in AxTrax NG, and try Find Panels again. IP Address Conflict If the panel was found and then stops responding, another device on the network may have been assigned the same IP address. Check your router's DHCP lease table and assign a static IP to the panel that falls outside the DHCP range. When to Replace the Panel If you have confirmed the IP settings are correct, there are no firewall or antivirus blocks, the firmware update still fails repeatedly, and the panel does not respond after a full power cycle, the panel's NIC (network interface) may have failed. Contact Rosslare Technical Support to initiate an RMA. Still Having Issues? Contact Rosslare Technical Support with: panel model and serial number, IP address of the panel and the AxTrax NG PC, screenshot of panel properties, ping test results, and AxTrax NG version (Help > About). Related Articles Article 1: Cards / Credentials Not Working in AxTrax NG Article 3: How to Move AxTrax NG to a New Computer Article 5: AxTrax NG — DB Disconnected or Client Not Connecting to Server How to Move AxTrax NG to a New Computer Overview This guide explains how to move AxTrax NG and its database from one Windows PC to another. This is needed when replacing a server PC, upgrading hardware, or recovering from a system failure. The process has four steps: back up the database on the old PC, install AxTrax NG on the new PC, restore the database, and verify the system is working. PC Minimum Requirements for the New Machine Step 1 — Back Up the Database on the Old PC Open AxTrax NG on the current (old) PC. Go to Tools > Database in the menu bar. Select Backup Now. Choose a destination you can access from the new PC — a USB drive, a shared network folder, or an external hard drive. Wait for the backup to complete. You will see a confirmation message when it finishes. Copy the backup file to the new PC (via USB or network share) before proceeding. Step 2 — Stop the Server Services Before Upgrading (Upgrade Only) If you are upgrading AxTrax NG on the same PC rather than moving to a new machine, you must stop the running services before launching the installer. Skip to Step 3 if you are doing a fresh install on a brand-new PC. Before running the installer, open Windows Task Manager (Ctrl + Shift + Esc) on the current PC if you are upgrading in place. On the Processes or Services tab, find and stop the AxtraxNG Server Monitor Process (also called AxtraxNG Watchdog). Also find and stop the AxtraxNG Server Service (AxtraxServerService). IMPORTANT: If these are not stopped before running the installer, the Server Monitor and AxtraxNG Server Service will not be upgraded to the new version. Step 3 — Install AxTrax NG on the New PC Download the latest AxTrax NG installer from www.axtraxng.com or use the installation media from your distributor. Extract the .ZIP file to a local folder on the new PC. Right-click the AxtraxNGSetup.....EXE file and select Run As Administrator. Do not simply double-click it. Check the I Agree checkbox to accept the license agreement. Select AxtraxNG Server with Server Monitor AND AxtraxNG Client. Both must be checked for a full server installation. Do NOT check AxTimeNG, ViTrax, or ViTrax LPR — these options are no longer supported. Click Start. Follow each subsequent screen by clicking Next, Install, or Finish. Do not change any file locations or other settings. When prompted about SQL Server, leave Install as Default selected (this installs SQL Server Express in its default configuration). Click OK. Wait for all components to finish installing. The process can take 10–15 minutes on a first install. Step 4 — Restore the Database on the New PC Open AxTrax NG on the new PC. Go to Tools > Database > Restore. Browse to the backup file you copied from the old PC and select it. If prompted about version compatibility or an earlier version format, choose to proceed with the restore. Wait for the restore to complete. This may take several minutes depending on database size. Once complete, restart AxTrax NG. All panels, cardholders, and access groups should be present. Step 5 — Verify the System Is Working Go to AC Networks in AxTrax NG and check the status of all panels. Ensure the new PC's IP address is set as a static IP and that it is in the same subnet as the panels. If panels show as offline, add a Windows Firewall rule for TCP port 10001 on the new PC. Right-click each panel and select Test Connection, or run Find Panels to re-establish communication. After panels come online, right-click the network and select Send All Data to Panels to push all settings to the hardware. After upgrading, always push a firmware update to all panels (select each panel, click Update Firmware icon) to ensure panel firmware matches the new software version. Setting Up a Client PC (Optional) If other PCs need access to AxTrax NG, install the Client version on each. During setup, select AxtraxNG Client only (do not check the Server option). Use the AxTrax NG Config Tool (installed with the client, found in the Start menu) to enter the IP address of the server PC. All client and server installations must run the exact same version of AxTrax NG. Common Issues After Migration Login fails after restore: Use the default admin password (1234). If that does not work, contact Rosslare Support for a password reset procedure. Panels show offline: The new PC likely has a different IP address than the old one. Set a static IP, add a firewall rule for TCP port 10001, confirm panels are reachable via ping, and run Find Panels. Restore fails with a version error: Install the same version of AxTrax NG that was used to create the backup, restore the database, then upgrade to the newer version. Cards or access groups are missing after restore: Verify the backup file is from the correct system. If the backup appears incomplete, contact Rosslare Support. Still Having Issues? Contact Rosslare Technical Support at support.na@rosslaresecurity.com with your AxTrax NG version (Help > About) and the error message seen during backup or restore. Related Articles Article 5: AxTrax NG — DB Disconnected or Client Not Connecting to Server Article 2: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG Standalone Controller Programming — Factory Reset, Code Enrollment, and Setup Overview Rosslare standalone controllers operate independently — no PC or network connection is required. All programming is done directly on the device using the keypad or a credential reader. This article covers entering programming mode, performing a factory reset when the code is unknown, enrolling access credentials, and configuring the unlock relay. Default Programming Codes by Model Series Entering Programming Mode Locate the programming code for your controller. The factory default is listed in the table above. Enter the programming code on the keypad. The controller will emit a distinct beep or LED sequence to confirm you are now in programming mode. If the programming code is unknown or has been changed and lost, perform a Factory Reset (see section below) to restore the default code before proceeding. Factory Reset — When the Programming Code Is Unknown If the programming code has been lost or changed by a previous installer, perform a factory reset to restore the unit to defaults. Power off the controller by disconnecting the power supply. Locate the reset input or tamper switch on the controller board (refer to the model's quick-start guide for the exact location). While holding the reset switch, reconnect the power supply. Continue holding for 5–10 seconds until you hear a beep or see the LED flash, indicating the reset is complete. Release the switch. The controller will restart with factory default settings and the default programming code. Enroll new credentials and set a new unlock code immediately after resetting. Enrolling Credentials (Cards or PIN Codes) Enter programming mode using the programming code. Follow the enrollment prompt for your model. On most Rosslare standalone controllers, presenting a card or entering a PIN while in programming mode will enroll that credential. The controller will beep once to confirm successful enrollment. Repeat for each credential you need to enroll. Exit programming mode (usually by entering the programming code again or waiting for the timeout period). Test each enrolled credential to confirm access is granted. Changing the Unlock Code or Configuring the Auxiliary Relay To change the door unlock code or configure when the auxiliary relay fires (e.g., to trigger a gate opener or alarm output): Enter programming mode. Navigate to the unlock code / auxiliary relay menu. On Q-series controllers, this is Menu Option 9. Enter and confirm the new unlock code. Exit programming mode and test the unlock code at the door. If the lock is operating in reverse (unlocking when it should be locked), enter a toggle code to switch the relay behavior — this is often caused by the relay being normally-open vs. normally-closed wiring. Configuring Bypass / Free Access Mode To set the controller to grant free access during certain hours (for example, during business hours), enter programming mode and navigate to the schedule or bypass menu. The exact menu sequence varies by model — refer to your model's quick-start guide or contact Technical Support for the specific steps for your unit. Changing PIN Length The AC-G4x and similar models allow PIN length to be adjusted between 4 and 8 digits. Enter programming mode and navigate to the PIN length setting. Set the PIN length before enrolling codes to ensure all codes are the same length. Credential Does Not Respond in Programming Mode If you present a credential during programming mode and nothing happens: Confirm you successfully entered programming mode — you should have heard the programming-mode entry beep. Try a full factory reset. A previously enrolled programming card may be blocking keypad entry. If the controller still does not respond to credentials during enrollment after a factory reset, the unit may be defective. Contact Rosslare Technical Support for an RMA evaluation. Still Having Issues? Contact Rosslare Technical Support with the controller model number and serial number (found on the label on the back of the device), along with a description of the behavior you are seeing. Related Articles Article 1: Cards / Credentials Not Working in AxTrax NG Troubleshooting: AxTrax NG — DB Disconnected or Client Not Connecting to Server Overview If AxTrax NG opens with a 'DB Disconnected' message or a client PC cannot connect to the server, this article covers the four most common causes and how to fix them. This issue is almost always caused by the AxTraxNG Server service not running, a version mismatch between the client and server, the wrong server IP address configured on the client, or the Server component never having been installed. Understanding the AxTrax NG Components AxTrax NG has three main components — understanding what each does helps diagnose connection issues faster: AxTraxNG Client: The user interface where all configuration changes are made. Can be installed on multiple PCs. AxTraxNG Server (AxtraxServerService): Runs as a Windows background service. It acts as the bridge between SQL Server, the access control panels, and the Client interface. This service must be running at all times. It is set to start automatically on system startup. AxTraxNG Server Monitor (formerly Watchdog): Runs in the Windows system tray. Provides a visual indicator of the Server Service status and starts automatically with Windows. If the Server Monitor icon is missing from the tray, the service may have stopped. Common Causes and Solutions Cause 1 — AxTraxNG Server Service Is Not Running This is by far the most common cause of DB Disconnected. The service can stop after a PC restart, Windows update, or unexpected shutdown. Press Windows + R, type services.msc, and press Enter to open Windows Services. Scroll down and find AxtraxNG Server (also listed as AxtraxNG Server Service or AxtraxServerService). Check the Status column. If it says Stopped, right-click it and select Start. Wait a few seconds for the service to fully start. Open AxTrax NG — the DB Disconnected message should clear. Also check the AxTraxNG Watchdog (also called AxTraxNG Server Monitor) in the system tray. This utility monitors the server service and shows ongoing status. If it is not running, locate it in the Start menu and launch it. If the service starts but immediately stops again, open Event Viewer (eventvwr.msc) > Windows Logs > Application and look for AxTrax-related errors to identify the root cause. Cause 2 — Client and Server Are Running Different Versions AxTrax NG requires the exact same version to be installed on all PCs — server and all clients. A minor version mismatch will prevent any client from connecting. On the server PC, open AxTrax NG and note the version (Help > About). On each client PC, open AxTrax NG and note the version the same way. All PCs must run the exact same version of AxTrax NG. A mismatch of even a minor version (e.g., 27.7.1.20 vs. 27.7.1.21) prevents the client from connecting. To fix a version mismatch, uninstall AxTrax NG on the client PC, install the same version as the server, and test the connection. Do not upgrade the server without also upgrading all clients to the same version at the same time. Cause 3 — Client Has the Wrong Server IP Address When AxTrax NG is installed as a client, it needs the IP address of the server PC. If the server's IP has changed or was entered incorrectly during setup, the client cannot connect. On the client PC, locate and open the AxTrax NG Config Tool (Start menu > Rosslare > AxTraxNG Config Tool, or in C:\Program Files (x86)\Rosslare\). Find the Server IP Address or Server Name field. Enter the IP address of the Windows PC that has the AxTraxNG Server installed. To find the server PC's IP, go to the server PC, open Command Prompt, type ipconfig, and note the IPv4 Address. Save the settings and restart AxTrax NG on the client. It should now connect. Cause 4 — AxTraxNG Server Component Was Never Installed If AxTrax NG was installed using the Client only option, there is no local server or database to connect to. The Server component must be installed on one dedicated PC. On the PC showing DB Disconnected, go to the AxTrax NG installation folder (typically C:\Program Files (x86)\Rosslare\). Verify the Server component is installed. Look for AxtraxNG Server in Windows Services (services.msc). If it is not listed, the Server was never installed. Run the AxTrax NG installer again. When prompted for installation type, make sure AxtraxNG Server with Server Monitor is checked alongside AxtraxNG Client. After installation, restart the PC and open AxTrax NG. The DB Disconnected message should be resolved. Perform a database restore (Tools > Database > Restore) if you have a backup from the previous installation. Server vs. Client Installation — Quick Reference Still Having Issues? Contact Rosslare Technical Support at support.na@rosslaresecurity.com with: AxTrax NG version numbers from both server and client PCs, screenshot of the error, and any relevant entries from Windows Event Viewer > Application logs. Related Articles Article 3: How to Move AxTrax NG to a New Computer Article 2: Panel Not Connecting in AxTrax NG

files.content: # KB Article Enhancement Summary **Date:** April 30, 2026 **Status:** Complete — All 3 articles enhanced with official documentation references --- ## Enhancement Overview The three KB articles were verified and enhanced using official Rosslare documentation from your workspace: 1. ✅ **KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md** 2. ✅ **KB_02_Firmware_Updates.md** 3. ✅ **KB_03_Card_Credential_Management.md** --- ## Documentation Sources Used ### AxtraxNG_Site_Reference.md - Panel models and specifications (AC-215/225/425/825IP) - Network requirements (Ethernet vs RS-485 distances) - Software version details (AxtraxNG 27.7.1.20 final, AxtraxPro 28.0.5.2 current) - Firewall configuration requirements - System scale capabilities (up to 1,024 panels across 32 networks) - Card capacity by panel model ### firmware.pdf - Exact UI steps for firmware updates in AxtraxNG - Screenshot confirmation of the "incompatible firmware" warning dialog - Firmware update dialog details and settings - Progress monitoring via Downloads column and Events log - Status indicators (Download in progress, completion) - Error message interpretation ### AxtraxNG Installation & Support Bulletins - Network configuration best practices - Windows Firewall configuration procedures - IP address management - Remote client connectivity requirements --- ## Specific Enhancements ### Article 1: Panel Connectivity & Network Issues **Added Technical Specifications:** - Network cable distance limits (330 ft Ethernet, 2,000 ft RS-485) - Cable type specifications (Cat5e/Cat6 for Ethernet, 22 AWG 120Ω STP for RS-485) - System scale reference (up to 1,024 panels supported) **Enhanced Troubleshooting:** - Specific Windows Firewall configuration steps - ARP/UDP broadcast requirements - Static IP address recommendation - Remote vs. local network connectivity clarification **Improved Prevention:** - Windows Firewall enablement requirement - Static IP configuration emphasis - Regular connectivity testing guidance --- ### Article 2: Firmware Updates & Installation **Added Panel Model Reference:** - Supported models with capacity details: - AC-215 / AC-215IP (2-door) - AC-225 / AC-225IP (2-4 door, Series A/B) - AC-425 / AC-425IP (4-8 door, Series B) - AC-825IP (4-8 door with expansion) - Legacy Series A hardware note **Enhanced Step-by-Step Process:** - Exact software menu path: Right-click panel > Firmware Update - Official dialog handling: "Do NOT change any settings" - Warning message clarification: Click "Yes" for incompatible firmware - Progress monitoring via "Downloads" column (e.g., "452 / 472") - Events log review for status updates **Improved Error Handling:** - Error message examples directly from official documentation - "Invalid Firmware" interpretation - "Wrong value" firmware version mismatch explanation **Added Software Version Reference:** - AxTraxNG 27.7.1.20 (final release) - AxtraxPro 28.0.5.2+ (current recommended) --- ### Article 3: Card & Credential Management **Added Panel Capacity Reference:** - AC-215/225 max: 30,000 users, 20,000 events - AC-425 max: 30,000 users, 20,000 events - AC-825IP max: 60,000 users, 500,000 events **Enhanced Facility Code Section:** - Added card format technical detail (26-bit standard vs. no-FC format) - Clarified facility code is a system identifier (0-255 range) - Expanded troubleshooting for capacity limits - Added data archiving guidance for high-volume systems **Improved Biometric Reader Guidance:** - Technical explanation of facility code + card ID binding - Configuration mode vs. demo mode distinction - Reader reconfiguration procedures --- ## Verification Checklist | Item | Status | Notes | |---|---|---| | **Panel models listed** | ✅ Verified | AC-215/225/425/825 confirmed in official docs | | **Network specifications** | ✅ Verified | 330ft Ethernet, 2,000ft RS-485 from reference | | **Firmware steps** | ✅ Verified | Matches firmware.pdf UI screenshots | | **Software versions** | ✅ Verified | AxtraxNG 27.7.1.20 & AxtraxPro 28.0.5.2 | | **Firewall config** | ✅ Verified | Windows Firewall required, AxtraxService.exe rules | | **Card capacity** | ✅ Verified | Per-model capacities from reference docs | | **Facility code format** | ✅ Verified | 26-bit standard, optional no-FC variant | --- ## Alignment with Ticket Data These articles address the top issues from your 287-ticket analysis: - **Panel Connectivity** (64 tickets) → Article 1 with expanded network troubleshooting - **Firmware Updates** (18 tickets) → Article 2 with exact UI steps and error handling - **Card Management** (22 tickets) → Article 3 with capacity planning and facility code guidance --- ## Notes for Implementation 1. **AxtraxNG End-of-Life:** Per official docs, AxtraxNG downloads end 12/31/2026, support ends 12/31/2027. Consider noting this for customers on older versions. 2. **AxtraxPro Migration:** Customers on AxtraxNG may need migration support. AC-825IP panels require MCU type VG for AxtraxPro compatibility. 3. **Windows Requirements:** Both AxtraxNG and AxtraxPro require Windows Firewall enabled. McAfee and Symantec antivirus are not supported. 4. **Firewall Configuration:** The manual `firewall.cmd` script in the installer directory can auto-configure firewall rules—mention this to users having connectivity issues. --- ## Next Steps (Optional) Consider creating additional KB articles for: - Reader configuration and troubleshooting (21 tickets in analysis) - Software installation and migration (17 tickets) - Schedule/timezone configuration (7 tickets) - Advanced network configuration (for RS-485 systems) - Database backup and recovery procedures --- ## Files Updated - `KB_01_Panel_Connectivity_Issues.md` (6.0 KB → 7.2 KB) - `KB_02_Firmware_Updates.md` (7.0 KB → 8.4 KB) - `KB_03_Card_Credential_Management.md` (9.7 KB → 11.5 KB) All articles now include official documentation references and verified technical specifications.
